title: Anil mauritanica

taxonomy: Anil mauritanica is classified under the family Apocynaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The genus Anil is a part of this family and is native to tropical Africa. Anil mauritanica is further classified under the order Gentianales, which includes other flowering plants like gentians and coffee. The species Anil mauritanica is a climbing plant with small white flowers and is often found in tropical forests. However, more research is needed to confirm the exact classification of Anil mauritanica.
preparations: Anil mauritanica preparations are used in traditional medicine, often in the form of teas, infusions, or decoctions.
